title: Geopolitics of LPG: Supply Security in 2026
speaker: Dr. Fatih Birol, IEA / James Rockall, WLGA
summary: The opening plenary addressed mounting geopolitical pressures reshaping global LPG trade flows. Dr. Fatih Birol highlighted the role of LPG in energy access and transition, noting that supply diversification is critical for import-dependent regions. James Rockall outlined WLGA's Supply Security Risk Framework and urged industry to prepare for disruption scenarios. Key themes included Middle East and North Africa as anchor suppliers, US exports as a balancing source, and the importance of storage and logistics resilience.
key_quote: LPG is not just an energy source — it is a public health intervention.
action_items: WLGA to publish a Supply Security Risk Framework document by Q3 2026

title: BioLPG & Renewable LPG: The Road to Net Zero
speaker: Sarah Mitchell, SHV Energy / Pierre Dubois, TotalEnergies
summary: Session focused on the scaling of BioLPG and renewable LPG (rLPG) as a pathway to decarbonise the industry. Sarah Mitchell presented SHV Energy's commitment to 100% rLPG in key markets by 2030. Pierre Dubois discussed TotalEnergies' production and certification efforts. Panellists agreed on the need for harmonised sustainability standards and green finance taxonomy inclusion. Certification schemes such as ISCC and RSB were referenced as critical for credibility.
key_quote: BioLPG is not a niche — it is the bridge to net zero for millions of off-grid users.
action_items: WLGA to convene a BioLPG certification working group; industry to align on a common sustainability reporting metric

title: Clean Cooking: Achieving Universal LPG Access by 2030
speaker: Rebecca Foster, AfDB / Raj Patel, Indian Oil / Sandra Okello, CCA
summary: The session explored progress toward universal access to clean cooking, with a focus on LPG. Rebecca Foster announced the African Development Bank's commitment to finance clean cooking projects across the continent, including LPG distribution and cylinder programmes. Raj Patel shared lessons from India's PMUY scheme. Sandra Okello emphasised the need for blended finance and government-industry partnerships. Nigeria, Kenya, and Ghana were highlighted as high-potential markets.
key_quote: We will not achieve SDG 7 without LPG at the table.
action_items: AfDB to launch a dedicated LPG clean cooking facility; WLGA to produce a Clean Cooking Investment Guide

title: Digital Transformation in the LPG Industry
speaker: Andreas Mueller, Westfalen / Elena Kowalski, Vitol
summary: Panellists discussed digital tools transforming LPG operations: IoT-enabled cylinders, demand forecasting, and trading platforms. Andreas Mueller showcased smart valve and tank monitoring solutions. Elena Kowalski spoke on digitalisation in trading and risk management. Topics included data standards, cybersecurity, and the role of AI in optimising supply chains. Consensus was that digital adoption will accelerate in the next five years.
key_quote: Data is the new barrel — whoever uses it best will win.
action_items: Industry to collaborate on a common data exchange standard for cylinder and tank tracking

title: LPG Safety Standards: Global Harmonisation
speaker: James Rockall, WLGA / Jean-Pierre Dupont, Hexagon Ragasco
summary: The session addressed the need for globally harmonised LPG safety standards to reduce accidents and facilitate trade. James Rockall presented WLGA's work with ISO and regional bodies. Jean-Pierre Dupont discussed composite cylinder standards and testing. Panellists called for alignment between UN, ISO, and regional norms to avoid fragmentation. Training and consumer awareness were emphasised as complementary to regulation.
key_quote: One standard, one test, one certificate — that is the goal.
action_items: WLGA to lead a harmonisation roadmap with ISO and key national standards bodies

title: LPG Financing & Investment Trends
speaker: Rebecca Foster, AfDB / Francois Leclerc, Mabanaft
summary: Financing and investment trends in LPG were discussed, with a focus on green finance and emerging markets. Rebecca Foster outlined AfDB's blended finance instruments for clean cooking and LPG infrastructure. Francois Leclerc shared the perspective of traders and midstream investors. ESG criteria and green bonds were highlighted as increasingly important for project finance. The session noted strong interest from development finance institutions in LPG in Africa and Asia.
key_quote: The capital is there — we need bankable projects and policy certainty.
action_items: WLGA to develop a template for LPG project bankability to attract DFI and private capital
